layout.title: Open-Q
layout.pilot_mode: Pilot Mode: Changes are temporary & data will not be saved

step_welcome.what: Review the study objectives and confirm your voluntary participation.
step_welcome.why: Ensures you are fully informed and have consented to the research process.

step_presort.what: Answer a few quick questions about your profile.
step_presort.why: Helps us understand how different perspectives correlate with participant backgrounds.

step_rough.what: Spontaneously sort the statements into three categories: Agree, Neutral, and Disagree.
step_rough.why: Captures your initial, intuitive reaction to the study's central themes.

step_fine.what: Distribute your statements on the grid based on your level of agreement.
step_fine.why: Creates a structured, measurable map of your specific point of view.

step_post.what: Review your final sort and provide additional reflections if you wish.
step_post.why: Allows you to clarify your perspective and enrich the data with qualitative detail.

login.title: Admin Login
login.subtitle: Enter your credentials to manage your studies
login.card_description: Secure access to your research dashboard
login.privacy: By signing in, you agree to our Terms and Privacy Policy.
login.error_generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.

register.title: Create Account
register.subtitle: Join the research project
register.success_desc: Your account has been created and linked to the study.
register.success_hint: You can now log in to the administrative dashboard to begin collaborating.
register.invalid_desc: This invitation link is invalid or has expired.
register.missing_token_desc: This page requires a valid invitation token. Please check the link you received.
register.privacy_hint: By registering, you agree to follow the research ethics guidelines configured for this study.

consent.label: I confirm that I have read the above information and consent to the processing of my data for the purposes of this study.
consent.error: Please accept the terms to continue.

welcome.reset_confirm: Are you sure you want to start a new session? This will clear your current progress.
welcome.default_instructions:
1. **Let's meet:** a few quick questions to better understand your background.
2. **First impressions:** discover the statements and give your immediate reaction (agree, neutral, or disagree).
3. **Your perspective:** refine your thoughts by placing each statement on the grid to reflect your exact viewpoint.
4. **Why:** a few words to explain your most significant choices.

profile.title: Let's meet
profile.description: A few quick questions to better understand your background.

rough.title: First impressions
rough.description: Discover the statements and give your immediate reaction (agree, neutral, or disagree).

fine.title: Your perspective
fine.description: Place the statements onto the grid to refine your point of view, prioritizing what matters most to you.

post.title: Why
post.description: A few words to explain your most significant choices.

consent.title: Informed consent and data processing agreement
consent.subtitle: Please read the following information carefully.
consent.default_text:
1. Nature of the study and participant understanding
By checking the box below, you acknowledge that you have reviewed the study objectives outlined on the home page. You understand that this exercise employs Q-methodology, a research technique designed to model subjective viewpoints. The task requires you to sort statements according to their significance relative to your personal perspective; consequently, there are no objective "right" or "wrong" answers.

2. Data processing and pseudonymization (GDPR compliance)

Data collection: Your responses (the Q-sort configuration and post-sort explanations) will be assigned a unique alphanumeric code upon submission.

De-identification and retention:

Standard procedure: Direct identifiers (such as IP addresses) are immediately converted into an anonymous code and are never stored in their original format. This technical process ensures that your digital footprint cannot be used to re-identify you once the session ends.

Exception for follow-up: If you voluntarily provide your contact details at the end of the study for a follow-up contact, the link between your identity (email) and your response will be maintained strictly for the duration of that specific follow-up phase.

Reporting: The final results will be presented in an anonymized format. Data will be aggregated to reveal shared social perspectives (factors). Qualitative comments may be quoted to contextualize these factors but will be screened to remove revealing details.

3. Voluntary participation and right to withdraw
Your participation is strictly voluntary. You maintain the right to discontinue the sorting process and close your browser at any time prior to submission without penalty.

Pre-submission: If you withdraw before finalizing your sort, no partial data will be retained.

Post-submission: Due to the statistical nature of Q-methodology (factor analysis), once your data has been submitted and aggregated into the dataset, individual withdrawal may not be feasible.

4. Data retention and rights
In accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you retain the right to request access to, or rectification of, your personal data as long as it remains identifiable (i.e., prior to final anonymization).

presort.title: Before we start
presort.description: Please answer a few questions to help us situate your viewpoint.

header.subtitle: Swipe **left** to Disagree, **right** to Agree, or **down** to be Neutral.
header.hint: This is spontaneous — you can change your mind right after.

complete.title: First Step Complete
complete.subtitle: We will now refine the nuances.

instructions.desktop_tip: Drag, press, or use keyboard ( ← ↓ → ) to sort

deck.title: Available Statements
deck.all_placed: All statements in this pile are sorted

workbench.drag_or_tap: Drag to grid or tap slot

methodology.extremes: Tip: Start with what is most obvious to you (the extremes).
methodology.vertical: In the same column, the order from top to bottom doesn't matter.
methodology.constraint: You have to fit everything! It can be a puzzle, but that's how we prioritize choices.
methodology.relative: Reminder: items are sorted relative to each other (relative agreement).
methodology.zoom: Feel free to zoom in on different parts of the grid for better visibility.
methodology.flexibility: Nothing is final: you can move cards or put them back in the deck at any time.

post.title: To conclude
post.description: You're almost done! Your comments on the most significant statements are crucial for our analysis.

extreme.why: Why did you choose to place this statement here? How is it significant to you?
extreme.placeholder: E.g., A specific event, a strong belief, or a particular word in the statement...
extreme.min_chars: A few words are enough to help us understand the context.

optional.title: Additional Comments (optional)
optional.description: Feel free to add comments to any other statement if you wish to elaborate on your choices.

post.validation_error: Please briefly explain your key choices to continue.

success.title: Thank you for your participation!
success.message: Your contribution to the study has been saved.

contact.title: Results & Contact
contact.interview_consent: I agree to be contacted by the researcher for follow-up.
contact.newsletter_consent: I wish to be informed about the study results.
contact.gdpr_note: Your email will never be publicly shared. It is stored solely for the purposes selected above.

guidance.public: Best for high-volume recruitment. A single URL for all participants.
guidance.individual: Maximum security. Generates unique links that expire after one successful completion.
guidance.limited: Controlled access. One link that stops working after a fixed number of submissions.

process_steps.desc: Define the phases shown to participants on the welcome page. If left empty, default steps will be used.

defaults.new_desc: Explain what the participant will do in this phase.

reset.process_steps_confirm: Are you sure you want to reset all process steps to defaults? This will replace your custom steps.

fields.title: Study title
fields.subtitle: Subtitle (optional)

fields.legal_placeholder: Enter the full legal agreement text...

intro.consent_desc: Participants must agree to these terms before starting.

condition.title: Condition of Instruction
condition.label: Define Condition of Instruction
condition.desc: This is the core prompt that guides participants throughout the sorting process.
condition.placeholder: e.g. Please rank the following statements from those you most agree with to those you most disagree with
condition.grid_desc: This is the core instruction guiding participants during the grid sorting process.
condition.pre_desc: Instruction given to participants during the initial rough sort.
condition.pre_placeholder: e.g. Based on your personal point of view; divide the cards into three piles...

tips.title: Why is this important?
tips.desc: In Q-methodology, the 'Condition of Instruction' determines the point of view from which the participant sorts the items. It should be clear, singular, and consistent across all sorting phases.

questions.enable_presort_desc: Collect demographic information or other data before the sorting task.

empty.title: No questions yet
empty.desc: Click above to add your first question

bulk.title: Bulk editor (quick paste)
bulk.desc: One statement per line. Supports "Code: Text" and Excel copy-paste (multi-language).
bulk.placeholder:
Simple: 
1. My statement

Expert (Excel paste with headers): 
code	en	fr
s1	Hello	Salut

set.title: Q-set
set.confirm_clear: Are you sure you want to delete ALL statements? This cannot be undone.
set.confirm_reset_codes: Are you sure you want to re-sequence all statement codes (s1, s2, s3...)?

settings.title: Research settings
settings.desc: Configure how statements are presented to participants
settings.show_codes_desc: Display statement codes (e.g., "S1", "S2") alongside the text.
settings.randomize_desc: Present statements in random order to each participant (consistent within session)

grid.title: Forced distribution grid
grid.desc: The grid shape forces participants to discriminate between statements, approximating a normal distribution.
grid.symmetry_lock_desc: Automatically apply changes to opposite columns to maintain a balanced distribution.
grid.auto_balance_desc: Reshape grid to a balanced semi-normal distribution
grid.reshaped: Grid reshaped to a balanced distribution
grid.no_statements: Add statements first to auto-shape the grid
grid.tooltip_desc: Q-methodology uses a quasi-normal distribution (kurtosis > 0) to ensure participants make meaningful distinctions. The pyramid shape prevents central tendency bias.
grid.locked_desc: This study is no longer in draft mode. Modifications are restricted to preserve data integrity for existing participants.
grid.locked_active_desc: The study is currently collecting data. To modify the design, you must first revert it to draft mode (warning: this may invalidate existing results).
grid.locked_paused_desc: The study is currently paused. Design remains locked to protect existing data.
grid.locked_closed_desc: This study is completed. The design is archived and cannot be changed.
grid.mismatch_desc: You have {{statements}} statements but the grid only has slots for {{slots}} items. Please adjust the columns below to match.
grid.unlock_hint: Go to Data Explorer to purge sessions and unlock structure

extreme.title: Targeted columns
extreme.desc: Select columns that trigger follow-up questions.
extreme.prompt_placeholder: Why did you place this statement here?

random_comments.title: Allow random comments
random_comments.desc: Allow participants to add comments to any statement in the grid.

custom.title: Custom questions
custom.desc: Add custom questions to the post-sort survey.

email.title: Participant Follow-up
email.desc: Collect email addresses for follow-up or research results.
email.results: Offer to subscribe to a mailing list about study outcomes
email.interview_warning: Note: agreeing to be contacted lifts anonymity for the researcher.
email.results_hint: Email solely for results, anonymity preserved.

interface.title: Interface customization
interface.desc: Customize the buttons and labels of the interface to match your study's tone (e.g., changing "Agree/Disagree" to "Like/Dislike").

terms.title: Sorting terminology
terms.desc: Define the labels for the spontaneous sort and the grid spectrum.

hints.title: Methodology Tips
hints.desc: Add or modify floating hints to help participants during the grid sort phase.

help.title: Step Guidance (What/Why)
help.desc: Customize the context-sensitive help available to participants at each step via the '?' overlay.

accent.title: Accent color

upload.invalid_type: Invalid file type. Use PNG, JPG, or SVG.
upload.drag_drop: Drag & drop or click to upload

csv.desc: Raw rectangular data. Best for R, Python, SPSS, or Excel analysis. Includes all metadata.

zip.title: PQMethod Bundle (ZIP)
zip.desc: Contains legacy .DAT and .STA files for DOS PQMethod analysis.

lifecycle.description: Archive the study to make it read-only for everyone.
lifecycle.notice: To archive a study, it must first be Closed. Once archived, no further changes can be made.

danger.delete_confirm: Are you sure? This action is IRREVERSIBLE.

settings.save_error_desc: Failed to update settings. Slug might be taken.
settings.archive_success_desc: The study is now archived and read-only.
settings.archive_error_desc: Failed to archive study. Is it closed?
settings.delete_success_desc: The study has been permanently removed.

settings.title: Workspace Settings

general.title: General Settings

general.slug_hint: Changing the slug will update all your research dashboard links.

settings.team_growth_desc: Need more researchers? You can invite collaborators to your workspace. They will be able to manage all studies within this workspace.

team.title: Team Members

team.remove_confirm: Are you sure you want to remove this member?

researcher.label: Researcher
